audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Judges / Shoftim Gaza gate, Delilah, capture, blinding, prison, Dagon temple spectacle, collapse, death, and burial narrative, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for sex-worker scene, Gaza ambush and gate removal, Delilah's payment arrangement, repeated binding tests, coercive relationship dynamics, weaving-text difficulty, Nazirite disclosure, hair/sign distinction, torture and acquired blindness, prison labor, public disability abuse, child guide, mixed civilian crowd, revenge prayer, self-involving mass killing, and family burial.

verses.restored: Samson went to Gaza and saw there a woman who was a sex worker, and he went in to her.
It was said to Gazites, Samson has come here. They surrounded and lay in wait for him all night at gate of city. They kept silent all night, saying, Until light of morning—then we will kill him.

Samson lay until half of night. He rose at half of night, grasped doors of gate of city and two doorposts, pulled them out with bar, set them upon his shoulders, and brought them up to top of hill that faces Hebron.
After this, he loved a woman in wadi of Sorek, and her name was Delilah.

Lords of Philistines came up to her and said to her, Entice him and see by what his strength is great, and by what we can prevail over him, so we may bind him to afflict/humiliate him. We each will give you eleven hundred silver.
Delilah said to Samson, Tell me, please, by what your strength is great, and with what you may be bound to afflict you.
Samson said to her, If they bind me with seven fresh bowstrings that have not been dried, I shall become weak and be like one human.
Lords of Philistines brought up to her seven fresh bowstrings that had not been dried, and she bound him with them.

The ambush was sitting for her in inner room. She said to him, Philistines are upon you, Samson! He snapped bowstrings as thread of flax is snapped when it smells fire, and his strength was not known.
Delilah said to Samson, Look, you have mocked me and spoken lies to me. Now tell me, please, with what you may be bound.
He said to her, If binding, they bind me with new ropes with which no work has been done, I shall become weak and be like one human.
Delilah took new ropes and bound him with them. She said to him, Philistines are upon you, Samson! The ambush was sitting in inner room, and he snapped them from upon his arms like a thread.
Delilah said to Samson, Until now you have mocked me and spoken lies to me. Tell me with what you may be bound. He said to her, If you weave seven locks of my head with web.
She fastened with pin and said to him, Philistines are upon you, Samson! He awoke from his sleep and pulled away pin of loom and web.
She said to him, How can you say, I love you, when your heart is not with me? These three times you have mocked me and have not told me by what your strength is great.
When she pressed him with her words all days and urged him, his soul/life was shortened to death.

He told her all his heart and said to her, A razor has not gone up over my head, because a Nazirite of Divine I am from womb of my mother. If I am shaved, my strength will turn aside from me, and I shall become weak and be like every human.

Delilah saw that he had told her all his heart. She sent and called lords of Philistines, saying, Come up this time, because he has told me all his heart. Lords of Philistines came up to her and brought silver in their hand.
She made him sleep upon her knees, called to a man, and he shaved seven locks of his head. She began to afflict/humiliate him, and his strength turned aside from upon him.
She said, Philistines are upon you, Samson! He awoke from his sleep and said, I will go out as time after time and shake myself free. He did not know that YHWH had turned aside from upon him.
Philistines seized him and gouged out his eyes. They brought him down to Gaza, bound him with double bronze fetters, and he was grinding in house of prisoners.
Hair of his head began to grow after it had been shaved.
Lords of Philistines gathered to sacrifice a great sacrifice to Dagon their god and for rejoicing. They said, Our god has given Samson our enemy into our hand.
The people saw him and praised their god, because they said, Our god has given into our hand our enemy, destroyer of our land, and one who multiplied our slain.
When their heart was good, they said, Call Samson, and let him make sport for us. They called Samson from house of prisoners, and he was made to perform before them. They stood him between pillars.
Samson said to boy who held him by his hand, Let me rest, and let me feel the pillars upon which house is established, so I may lean upon them.
House was full of men and women, and all lords of Philistines were there. Upon roof were about three thousand man and woman watching at Samson's performance.
Samson called to YHWH and said, My Lord YHWH, remember me, please, and strengthen me, please, only this time, Divine, so that I may take one vengeance from Philistines for my two eyes.
Samson grasped two middle pillars upon which house was established and leaned upon them, one with his right and one with his left.

Samson said, Let my life die with Philistines. He bent with strength, and house fell upon lords and upon all people who were in it. The dead whom he put to death in his death were more than those he put to death in his life.
His brothers and all house of his father came down, lifted him, brought him up, and buried him between Zorah and Eshtaol in tomb of Manoah his father. He had judged Israel twenty years.

translation_notes: The Gaza sex worker is not named or blamed. The verse reports Samson's visit without making her a symbol of sexual corruption, and it gives no permission to stigmatize sex workers.
Samson's removal of the Gaza gate is a feat of humiliation against a city and a display of power, not a model for trespass, property destruction, or invulnerability.

Delilah is named and is approached by Philistine rulers with a large payment. The text does not state her ethnicity, motives beyond the arrangement, or whether additional pressure was applied; avoid both misogynistic demonization and unsupported exoneration.

Delilah explicitly asks how Samson can be bound 'to afflict/humiliate' him. Samson remains through repeated binding attempts and lies repeatedly. The relationship is unsafe, manipulative, and coercive; neither danger nor abuse should be romanticized as passion.
Verses 13-14 are textually difficult and appear compressed in the Masoretic Text; Greek witnesses preserve additional weaving details. Later audit should compare witnesses.

'His soul was shortened to death' describes extreme emotional exhaustion under relentless pressure. It must not be used to excuse coercion, blame victims for disclosure, or normalize wearing down a partner's boundaries.

Samson's hair is the embodied sign of Nazirite consecration, not a magic object independent of covenant identity. The narrative says YHWH had turned aside, not that disability or shaved appearance makes a person spiritually empty.
The gouging of Samson's eyes is torture and deliberate disability-making. Acquired blindness is not shame, punishment inherent in disability, moral contamination, or loss of personhood.
Grinding in prison is forced labor. The later public performance is captivity abuse and disability humiliation, not harmless entertainment.
The boy holding Samson's hand functions as a guide. Receiving guidance or access support is not infantilization or evidence that a blind person lacks agency.

The temple contains rulers and a large mixed crowd of men and women, including people on the roof. The collapse is mass killing with likely noncombatants present; it cannot be reduced to a clean strike against military leaders.
Samson's prayer is explicitly revenge for his eyes. Divine response in a tragic narrative does not turn vengeance or mass death into a universal command.
'Let my life die with Philistines' is a self-involving act of mass killing. It must never be preached as noble suicide, holy martyrdom, proof of faith, or permission to die while harming others.
Samson's family retrieves and buries him, restoring kinship and burial dignity after public degradation. Burial does not erase the devastation of his final act.
safety_note: Maximum safeguards: Do not eroticize coercion, blame all women through Delilah, shame sex workers, equate blindness with spiritual failure, mock disability, or romanticize captivity. Do not glamorize revenge, mass killing, self-destruction, martyrdom, or suicide through Samson's death. This ending is tragedy: a tortured captive kills himself and a crowded building of people. It is not a template for personal, political, or religious action.
